---
title: "The Qualities of Effective OERs"
format: html
---

## The 5Rs

David Wiley’s [@RN70139] 5Rs describes what OER creators should allow with appropriate licensing:

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Retain

*Keep a copy*. Consider the digital preservation of the resource: how long will it be need and valid for; will it still be usable later in its lifecycle, or is it dependent on proprietary software; is it stored securely and away from physical harm (data can be destroyed in a local disaster!)?
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Reuse

*Make available for use in different contexts*. To be able to reuse all or parts of a learning resource can minimise wasteful practices and maximise impact of your work. Using modular and extensible approaches to OER creation can help towards this goal.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Revise

*Modify or adapt*. Ensure the work can be updated or reused in other contexts by considering any barriers to revision and mitigating them.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Remix

*Combine and modify alongside other materials*. Consider what resources already exist that you can combine with your OER and ensure your output allows others to do the same.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Redistribute

*Share with others*. Ensure the OER is unambiguously licensed and available in a findable and searchable repository for onward sharing.
:::

Research colleagues might be more familiar with the FAIR Principles [@RN68106]:

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Findable

Using metadata, stable URLs, and repository platforms.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Accessible

Free to retrieve and provided in formats that support accessibility standards.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Interoperable

Structured in formats readable across tools and platforms.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Reusable

Clear licensing and editable source files.
:::

These principles are best practice and ensure that a learning object is fully open, adaptable, and retrievable.

[![](images/clipboard-2765196364.png){fig-align="center" width="50"}](/OERs%20for%20creators/Creator-Licensing.html)
